seo.title: Gemini Omni Flash | Google Omni Video, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Gemini Omni Flash on MaxVideoAI for 720p text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-to-video, video editing and conversational refine workflows.

overview: Gemini Omni Flash is Google's preview Omni video model in MaxVideoAI for 720p clips up to 10 seconds, with text, image, reference-image, source-video edit and previous-interaction refine workflows.
pricingNotes: Gemini Omni Flash is a preview Google route. MaxVideoAI shows the exact customer quote before generation; the 720p preview route is currently displayed at $0.13/s.

hero.title: Gemini Omni Flash - Google Omni video generation and conversational editing
hero.intro: Generate from text, animate a source image, guide a video with multiple references, edit a short source clip, or refine a previous Omni result with its interaction id.
hero.badge: 720p · up to 10s · Text, image, references, video edit & refine

secondaryLinks.label: Compare Gemini Omni Flash vs Veo 3.1

technicalOverview.label: Workflows
technicalOverview.body: Text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-to-video, video edit and conversational refine

technicalOverview.label: Duration
technicalOverview.body: Up to 10 seconds per current preview generation

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text prompt, one source image, up to 10 reference images, short source video, or previous interaction id

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native sound generation is directed through prompt guidance

technicalOverview.label: Unsupported
technicalOverview.body: No negative prompt, seed, first/last frame, extend, non-video output or 4K path in the current Omni preview route

promptStructure.title: Prompt mini-guide
promptStructure.quote: Subject + action + source/reference rule + camera direction + sound direction + what must stay stable.
promptStructure.description: Gemini Omni Flash works best when the main visual brief is concise and sound, camera and edit directions are separated.
steps: Start with one subject and one visual action.
State whether the run is text, image, reference, source-video edit or refine.
Name what must stay stable from the source or reference assets.
Add ambience, music, speech or SFX direction.
Use 16:9 or 9:16 and keep the shot within 10 seconds.

tips.title: Tips and limits
items: Keep Store interaction enabled when a result may need a follow-up refine pass.
Use reference-to-video when product identity, wardrobe or style must stay consistent.
For source-video edits, describe what stays before describing what changes.
Use sound direction as a short director note, not a long soundtrack script.
Choose Veo 3.1 instead when you need first/last-frame, extend or higher-resolution delivery.

compareLink.label: Compare Gemini Omni Flash vs Veo 3.1 ->

prompts.title: Conversational product refine
prompts.prompt: A premium coffee machine on a marble counter, slow dolly in, warm morning light, preserve product shape, subtle cafe ambience. Follow-up edit: tighten the product reveal and make the steam more visible.

prompts.title: Reference-led launch shot
prompts.prompt: Use the reference images to preserve the sneaker silhouette and colorway. Create a 9:16 launch clip with a clean studio turntable move, soft fabric foley and a short rising music bed.

prompts.title: Source-video edit
prompts.prompt: Keep the original camera move and timing from the source clip. Change the room into a bright editorial showroom, preserve the subject motion, add soft room tone and no subtitles.

faqs.q: Is Gemini Omni Flash available through Vertex AI on MaxVideoAI?
faqs.a: Yes. MaxVideoAI implements Gemini Omni Flash as a Google Vertex / Agent Platform Interactions route when the preview route is enabled for the account.

faqs.q: What is Gemini Omni Flash best for?
faqs.a: Use it for 720p short videos where text, image references, source-video edits and follow-up conversational refine matter more than 4K delivery or first/last-frame control.

faqs.q: How is it different from Veo 3.1?
faqs.a: Omni Flash is better positioned for stateful interaction and broader reference/edit workflows. Veo 3.1 remains the stronger page to evaluate first/last-frame, extend and higher-resolution Veo delivery paths.

faqs.q: Can Gemini Omni Flash generate audio?
faqs.a: Yes. Sound is directed through prompt guidance for ambience, music, speech or SFX, subject to the current preview route.

faqs.q: Does Gemini Omni Flash support 4K or 1080p?
faqs.a: No. The current MaxVideoAI Omni preview route is documented and exposed as 720p output.

custom.heroSupportLine: Best for stateful refine, reference-guided shots and short source-video edits.
custom.specNote: Specs describe the MaxVideoAI preview route for Gemini Omni Flash through Google Vertex Interactions.

specSections.title: Supported routes
specSections.intro: Gemini Omni Flash is exposed as a multimodal video route rather than a Veo-style long-running prediction route.
items: Reference-to-video with up to 10 reference images.
Video edit from a short source video.
Conversational refine using a previous interaction id.

specSections.title: Boundaries
items: No negative prompt or seed controls.
No 1080p or 4K output in the current preview route.
No public provider implementation guide on this marketing page.

custom.finalPara1: Gemini Omni Flash is the Google Omni route to test when stateful editing and references matter more than traditional Veo delivery controls.

hero.title: Gemini Omni Flash
hero.subtitle: Stateful video editing, up to 10s, 720p output, reference images, source-video edits and native sound direction in one Google Omni workflow.
hero.paragraph: Use Gemini Omni Flash when the job is not only one prompt-to-video render: start from text, a source image, up to 10 reference images, a short source video, or a previous interaction id when you want a conversational refine pass.

features.title: Stateful refine
features.body: Store the interaction id and continue the same Omni output in a follow-up edit.

features.title: Reference stack
features.body: Guide the scene with one image or up to 10 reference images.

features.title: Video edit
features.body: Upload a short source clip and describe the change, camera direction and sound direction.

features.title: Native sound direction
features.body: Give ambience, music, speech or SFX instructions inside the prompt.

features.title: Preview limits
features.body: Current Google preview constraints are 720p, 16:9 or 9:16, and up to 10 seconds.

features.title: Vertex route
features.body: MaxVideoAI keeps the implementation on the Google Vertex Interactions path.

decisionCards.title: Omni Flash or Veo 3.1?
decisionCards.body: Choose Omni Flash for conversational refine, source-video edits and larger reference stacks. Choose Veo 3.1 when you need the mature Veo route for first/last-frame, extend or higher-resolution delivery.

decisionCards.title: Need a refine workflow?
decisionCards.body: Keep Store interaction enabled when the output may need follow-up edits. The saved interaction id becomes the bridge for the next Omni pass.

decisionCards.title: Writing prompts?
decisionCards.body: Keep the main prompt short, then add separate sound, camera and edit directions so the UI can preserve them across modes.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Start with one clear subject, one action, sound direction and the 16:9 or 9:16 output shape.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Use one source image when the opening composition or product shape matters.

referenceWorkflows.title: Reference-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Use multiple references for identity, wardrobe, product form, palette or scene style.

referenceWorkflows.title: Video edit
referenceWorkflows.body: Upload one short clip and state what must stay before describing what should change.

referenceWorkflows.title: Conversational refine
referenceWorkflows.body: Reuse the previous interaction id for follow-up changes instead of rebuilding the shot.

demo.title: Gemini Omni Flash demo prompt
demo.prompt:
Prompt: Golden-hour rooftop above a modern city. Two friends discover a small handheld recorder can turn spoken memories into warm moving light. One friend presses record; translucent images of a childhood beach form briefly in the air between them, then dissolve in the wind. Their faces shift from curiosity to wonder. Premium cinematic realism, warm backlight, no text or logos.
Sound direction: Soft rooftop wind, recorder click, distant city ambience, gentle ocean echo as the memory appears, one whispered line: "That was my favorite day."
Camera direction: Smooth lateral dolly ending on their surprised faces, 50mm lens feel, shallow depth of field, golden-hour backlight.

summary.subject: Two friends on a golden-hour rooftop
summary.action: A recorder turns a memory into moving light
summary.camera: Smooth lateral dolly ending on a two-face reaction
summary.style: Premium cinematic realism, warm backlight, soft city atmosphere
summary.output: Rooftop wind, recorder click, ocean echo, one whispered line
